\textit{Source: Stacks 01CR (internal hom of modules); project-bespoke restriction comparison.}
For an open immersion $f:Y\to X$ and $M\in X.\mathrm{Modules}$, there is an isomorphism
$(\dual M)|_Y \cong \dual(M|_Y)$ of $\mathcal{O}_Y$-modules, natural in $M$. For the downstream
application (local triviality of a line bundle's dual) only the restriction of this comparison to
opens $V\subseteq U$ contained in the image of $f$ is needed.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
\uses{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}
This is the classical fact ``restriction to an open commutes with internal Hom'',
$f^*\mathcal{H}\!om(A,B)\cong\mathcal{H}\!om(f^*A,f^*B)$, specialised to $B=\mathcal{O}$. It is
built \emph{objectwise at the presheaf-of-modules level}, where the comparison lives.

For an open immersion, pushforward restricted to opens $V\subseteq U$ (with $U$ the image of $f$)
is literally evaluation: $(\mathrm{pushforward}\,f)(F)(V)=F(V)$, no reindexing. Hence on such $V$
both $(\dual M)|_Y$ and $\dual(M|_Y)$ evaluate to the \emph{same} $\mathcal{O}(V)$-module of
$\mathcal{O}|_V$-linear maps $M|_V\to\mathcal{O}|_V$, and the comparison map is the identity on
values, packaged with its $\mathcal{O}(V)$-linearity and naturality in $V$. The slice-level ends
defining each value match because the poset $\mathrm{Opens}$ is thin (hom-sets are
$\mathrm{Subsingleton}$, killing all $\mathrm{Over}$-coherence). The sectionwise ring-iso
transport carrying the $\mathcal{O}_X(fV)$-module structure to the $\mathcal{O}_Y(V)$-module
structure is the ModuleCat-level shadow \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}.

\emph{Route note (iter-230, settled empirically).} Do \emph{not} discharge this via the
fixed-value-category sheaf-site equivalence \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}: that root
is parametric in a fixed value category and does not transport the varying-ring
$\mathcal{O}(V)$-module fibration this comparison carries (the residual lives at the
presheaf-of-modules, not sheaf, level). The objectwise route above is the correct, minimal one and
avoids that detour entirely.
\end{proof}
